title: CITY OF PORTLAND WATER DISCOUNT PROGRAM - 211info
text: SELF ENHANCEMENT INC COMMUNITY & FAMILY PROGRAMS CITY OF PORTLAND WATER DISCOUNT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers sewer, stormwater, and water bill discount program. Households must reapply for assistance every two years. Applicants with an Accessory Dwelling Unit (ADU) on their property with one water meter that serves both the primary home and the ADU are not eligible to participate in the discount program. Water and sewer customers: -Households earning less than 60% of the median family income (MFI) may receive a discount of up to $210.35 on a 90-day bill. -Households earning less than 30% of MFI may receive a discount of up to $336.58 on a 90-day bill. -Sewer only or customers on alternative billing cycles discount amount will vary. 310 NE Failing Street Portland, OR 97212 Eligibility: City of Portland Water single-family residential accounts. The account holder must reside at the property. Customers with an outstanding balance are still eligible to apply. Household income must not exceed 60% of the state median family income (MFI) Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 713-5590 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Energy Assistance Hotline (503) 713-5590 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: -Proof of service address -Names and birthdates of all household members age 60 and older -Total gross monthly income for all household members over 18 -Proof of gross household income for last 30 days AREA SERVED: City of Portland SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Services: Discounted Sewer Service Discounted Water Service Other Services Offered At This Location: HOUSING/SHELTER - SHORT TERM RENTAL ASSISTANCE INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - CHILD WELFARE ADVOCACY MUTUAL SUPPORT - ANTI DOMESTIC AND SEXUAL VIOLENCE PROGRAM UTILITIES - ENERGY ASSISTANCE If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
